key_insight: Tree-based machine learning models can accurately predict halide perovskite band gaps from atomic and structural descriptors, revealing which site-specific features matter most.
engineering_relevance: These models can speed up screening of lead-free perovskites and guide composition design for optoelectronic materials with target band gaps.
method: Trained ensemble tree-based regressors: Random Forest, GBRT, and XGBoost.
Used atomic and structural descriptors across multiple halide perovskite families.
Interpreted feature importance to identify dominant B-site and X-site chemistry effects.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports strong predictive accuracy across diverse halide perovskite compositions and successful feature-importance analysis.
watch_item: Model performance may depend on the coverage and quality of the training data, so extrapolation to unseen chemistries should be treated cautiously.
paper_title: Machine learning insights into band gap properties in halide-based perovskites
